notes.content: Chemistry studies the composition of matter, its structure, and the means by which it is converted from one form to another. The general B.S. degree in Chemistry is the one chemistry program offered by our department that is certified by the American Chemical Society (ACS) . Students in this program pursue a strong foundation in math and physics, in addition to chemistry, taking the higher-level sequences of all course options. This degree provides a strong foundation in experimental processes, instrumentation, and quantitative analysis. Students will be well-prepared to apply their chemistry knowledge to a wide array of applications, including environmental, pharmaceutical, materials, and industrial chemistry.
notes.contentHtml:
Chemistry studies the composition of matter, its structure, and the means by which it is converted from one form to another.


The general B.S. degree in Chemistry is the one chemistry program offered by our department that is certified by the American Chemical Society (ACS). Students in this program pursue a strong foundation in math and physics, in addition to chemistry, taking the higher-level sequences of all course options. This degree provides a strong foundation in experimental processes, instrumentation, and quantitative analysis. Students will be well-prepared to apply their chemistry knowledge to a wide array of applications, including environmental, pharmaceutical, materials, and industrial chemistry.

notes.content: Graduates will be able to successfully pursue their career objectives in advanced education in professional and/or graduate schools, a scientific career in government or industry, a teaching career in the school systems or other related career tracks.
notes.contentHtml: Graduates will be able to successfully pursue their career objectives in advanced education in professional and/or graduate schools, a scientific career in government or industry, a teaching career in the school systems or other related career tracks.

notes.content: This major is not currently screening for major preparation at the time of admission; however, it is strongly encouraged that you complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer. Incomplete foundational coursework may delay your ability to graduate on time. Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .

This major is not currently screening for major preparation at the time of admission; however, it is strongly encouraged that you complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer. Incomplete foundational coursework may delay your ability to graduate on time.

 

Visit Department agreements as needed

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, this major requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA, with no grade lower than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
notes.contentHtml: At the time of TAG submission, this major requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA, with no grade lower than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
